genre_preference.value: mixed (likes fantasy, dislikes poorly executed narratives)
evidence: "What a waste of great talent. Potentially interesting premise, but terribly told."
"I LOVE this movie and lost count how many times I've watched it!"
"Disjointed, dead-end scenes; things there that didn’t need to be and things missing that should’ve been explained."
"The acting, dialogue, and scriptwriting is atrocious and ruins the movie."
"It's fast moving, funny, romantic, and happy."
"I was sorely disappointed in how this disaster went."
"You could tell that even the actors struggled to make it connect, not because of their skill level, but because it wasn’t their job to hold all the loose ends."
"If they had Oscars for worst actress, worst director, worst cinematography and worst editing, this movie would sweep them."
"Lee Pace as the pie maker is completely charming and I can't believe this is the same actor who plays Thranduil in The Hobbit."
"So glad to hear the series is coming back next year! I just discovered Lee Pace and Pushing Daisies."
"It’s eye-opening, visually-stunning, and simply amazing what these families do."
"I can't believe that a few questionable reviews will cheat us out of the planned 5 sequels."

summary: The user has a strong preference for fast-paced narratives with high production quality, often appreciating visual and auditory elements. They express disappointment in poorly executed stories, indicating a desire for well-crafted plots and character development.
